title: Medical Device Safety: MedlinePlus
content: Medical Device Safety On this page Basics Summary Start Here Learn More Related Issues Specifics See, Play and Learn No links available Research Clinical Trials Journal Articles Resources Find an Expert For You Patient Handouts Summary A medical device is any product used to diagnose, cure, or treat a condition, or to prevent disease. They range from small and simple, like a blood glucose meter, to large and complicated, like a ventilator. You might use one at home or at work, or you may need one in a hospital. To use medical devices safely: Know how your device works. Keep instructions close by Understand and properly respond to device alarms Have a back-up plan and supplies in the event of an emergency Keep emergency numbers available and update them as needed Educate your family and caregivers about your device Food and Drug Administration Start Here Medical Device Safety (Food and Drug Administration) Related Issues Medical Device Safety Action Plan: Protecting Patients, Promoting Public Health (Food and Drug Administration) Medical Device Safety Communications (Food and Drug Administration) MedWatch: The FDA Safety Information and Adverse Event Reporting Program (Food and Drug Administration) Specifics Full-Body CT Scans: What You Need to Know (Food and Drug Administration) Home Healthcare Medical Devices: A Checklist (Food and Drug Administration) Safely Using Sharps (Needles and Syringes) at Home, at Work and on Travel (Food and Drug Administration) Clinical Trials ClinicalTrials.gov: Equipment and Supplies (National Institutes of Health) Journal Articles References and abstracts from MEDLINE/PubMed (National Library of Medicine) Article: Advancing Public Health Through Internationally Coordinated Medical Device Registries Comment on...
